title: Coelogyne pollucifera
intro: Coelogyne pollucifera, also known as the "pollucifer orchid," is a species of orchid native to the Himalayan and Eastern Asian regions. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Ayurveda and Unani practices, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne pollucifera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne pollucifera is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Coelogyne pollucifera belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristic orchid flowers and growth habits. Within the Orchidaceae family, Coelogyne pollucifera is part of the subtribe Coelogyninae. The Coelogyninae subtribe is a group of orchids that are mostly found in Asia, particularly in the tropical regions. The specific classification of Coelogyne pollucifera includes its genus and species, making it a unique member of the Orchidaceae family.
uses: Coelogyne pollucifera is reportedly used in traditional medicine for its potential antibacterial and antifungal properties.
